title: Cota fulvida
taxonomy: Cota fulvida belongs to the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as sunflowers or daisies. This classification places Cota fulvida within the broader category of composites, which are characterized by the presence of florets arranged in a distinctive pattern. The genus Cota is a group of annual or biennial plants, and Cota fulvida is a specific species within this genus. Within the family Asteraceae, Cota fulvida is a member of the subfamily Carduoideae, which comprises thistle-like plants. Further classification of Cota fulvida can be found at the species level, but the above information provides a general understanding of its taxonomic position.

Cota fulvida, a flowering plant within the genus Cota, is a perennial herbaceous plant that produces daisy-like flowers. The plant has been found to possess health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer.
